input.background: Calculate the standard deviations of the batches of numbers provided in different units (years, months, days) and determine which batch has the greatest variability.
input.question: Which batch has the greatest variability?

output.answer: The distributions are identical and are merely expressed in different units.

batch_years.value: Values of the batch provided in years (not explicitly provided in the question, assumed to be a list of numerical values)
batch_years.description: A batch of numbers representing data in years.

batch_months.value: Values of the batch provided in months (not explicitly provided in the question, assumed to be a list of numerical values)
batch_months.description: A batch of numbers representing data in months.

batch_days.value: Values of the batch provided in days (not explicitly provided in the question, assumed to be a list of numerical values)
batch_days.description: A batch of numbers representing data in days.

standard_deviation.value: The calculated standard deviations for each batch (years, months, days).
standard_deviation.description: The measure of variability for each batch of numbers.

greatest_variability_batch.value: The batch (years, months, or days) with the highest standard deviation.
greatest_variability_batch.description: The batch with the greatest variability based on standard deviation.

meta_info.caption: Units of measure changes numeric values of a standard deviation.
